title: Verify an FFT implementation against a reference DFT
description: Cross-check a custom FFT against a naive DFT and an inverse round trip before trusting it in a pipeline.
body: Use when integrating a hand-written or hardware FFT into a signal chain. Do not assume a fast transform is correct just because it runs. Actions: run the FFT and a direct DFT on identical random inputs, compare bin-by-bin within tolerance, then verify forward-then-inverse reconstructs the input. Validation signals include maximum bin error below the floating-point noise floor and an energy check confirming Parseval's relation holds. Risk boundary: a transform that fails Parseval feeds wrong spectra downstream, so block release and inspect twiddle-factor indexing before continuing.
